title: Thermal management of electronics using co-located microjet nozzles and electronic elements
abstract: An electronics assembly in which microjet nozzles are co-located with electronic elements on the same substrate or layer. This technique may be used to integrate lower power- density electronics onto an existing microjet nozzle plate to perform microjet nozzle cooling, which removes the need for separate thermal management solutions for systems that contain both high and lower power-density elements in proximity. This technique may also be used in multilayered 3D integrated electronic substrates, allowing for thin, high performing thermal management solutions in 3D integrated stackups using microjets.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An electronics assembly, comprising: a first layer with a thickness; at least one active, heat-generating, electronic element disposed on or in the first layer; and at least one passageway that passes entirely through the thickness of the first layer, wherein the passageway is configured to conduct fluid therethrough, to extract heat from the at least one electronic element. 2. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the at least one passageway comprises a microjet nozzle. 3. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the first layer comprises top and bottom surfaces, and wherein an electronic element is on the top surface of the first layer. 4. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the first layer comprises an electronics substrate. 5. The assembly of claim 1, further comprising a second layer that is spaced from the first layer. 6. The assembly of claim 5, further comprising at least one active, heat-generating, electronic element disposed on or in the second layer. 7. The assembly of claim 6, further comprising a manifold layer between the first and second layers that defines a reservoir that is configured to hold fluid. 8. The assembly of claim 7, wherein the at least one passageway that passes entirely through the thickness of the first layer comprises a microjet nozzle that is configured to form a microjet. 9. The assembly of claim 8, further comprising at least one passageway that passes entirely through the thickness of the second layer, wherein the passageway is configured to conduct fluid therethrough, to extract heat from the at least one electronic element disposed on or in the second layer. 10. The assembly of claim 7, wherein the manifold layer comprises a fluid outlet. 11. The assembly of claim 7, where the at least one passageway in the first layer is non- uniformly configured to provide more effective cooling to hot spots on the second layer. 12. The assembly of claim 6, further comprising at least one passageway that passes entirely through the thickness of the second layer, wherein the passageway is configured to conduct fluid therethrough, to extract heat from the at least one electronic element that is disposed on or in the second layer. 13. The assembly of claim 6, further comprising at least one structure spanning the spacing between the first and second layers such that the structure is in contact with the first and second layer. 14. The assembly of claim 13, where the at least one structure is configured to conduct heat. 15. The assembly of claim 13, where the at least one structure is configured to provide structural support of the electronics assembly. 16. The assembly of claim 13, where the at least one structure is configured to aid fluid routing. 17. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the at least one passageway that passes entirely through the thickness of the first layer comprises one or both of fluid-flow enhancement features and area-enhancement features within the passageway. 18. The assembly of claim 17, where the at least one passageway that passes entirely through the thickness of the first layer contains a chamfer. 19. The assembly of claim 17, wherein the at least one passageway that passes entirely through the thickness of the first layer defines a cross section that is not round. 20. The assembly of claim 17, wherein the at least one passageway that passes entirely through the thickness of the first layer defines a cross section with an irregular circumference.
